theme: E4 cycle 4 — add the Sparse 'Govern & trust' band to /network (design-spec Stage 2, per-cycle row 153), the FINAL band of the page. /network (bands 1-4) explained WHAT the network is and HOW to run a node, but never answered 'is this governed -- by whom, how do proposals work?'. That is the E1 30-persona RLP Gap #4 (Enterprise Architect / OSS Maintainer / Community Lead all flagged no reachable governance model, change process, or accountable steward). The band lands a governance trust-anchor (4 on-page trust facts + a 'Read the governance model' CTA -> /reference/governance-model/) and the public-good ethos close (shown-not-preached). Gap #4 is PARTIAL by design -- the full org/governance surface is E3. After this band /network is structurally complete (cycle 154 = /vaults/[slug] relationship-block polish).
register: Tokyo Night dark-first; sparse closing register (one idea = governed-in-the-open; high whitespace; the band LANDS the page, no new weight); honest-topology (every signal + CTA resolves to a real route -- /reference/governance-model/, /reference/specification/ facts, /community/; no invented governance dashboard); ethos shown-not-preached (the public-good close is one muted manifesto line, not a slogan; the governance FACTS carry the conviction); writing-guidelines AVOID honored (no marketing adjectives; sentences <=25 words; actor/subject named); doctrine dial ~55/45 (the ethos line = conviction, the governance facts = restraint).
operator_scope: Operator confirmed this cycle's two decisions at session open: (1) band depth = TRUST-STRIP + ETHOS CLOSE (not the minimal sparse anchor) -- a compact governance signal row + CTA pair + a one-line ethos close, mirroring the homepage 'The Standard' trust band; (2) COMMIT-ONLY -- verify locally + commit, deploy with the later E4 surface bundle (no SS_VERCEL_TOKEN rotation dependency). No new route. Band-5 scope (govern & trust) was pre-ratified at the E4 epic-open gate (cycle 150) via the design spec.

iii_7step.measure: The E1 decadal close (30-persona RLP) surfaced Gap #4: governance/security/cadence not reachable from the site. Enterprise Architect ('my first committee question is published governance model: RFCs, decision logs, named accountable parties? ... nothing on the page or in nav points there; I'd have to spelunk GitHub'); OSS Maintainer ('no named security contact, no release-cadence signal, no surfaced governance mechanism -- RFC/ADR process, decision logs'); Community Lead ('Governance is invisible ... the verb govern appears; the democracy that gives it meaning does not'). /network carried the verb but not the mechanism. Network-Legibility rubric item 2 (edge/governance honesty) was the load-bearing open dimension for the close.
iii_7step.orient: Pure assembly. The homepage 'The Standard' band (pages/index.astro .the-standard) already establishes the trust-anchor device -- a row of .meta-tag fact pills + a CTA pair -- so band 5 is that pattern tilted from spec-only to GOVERNANCE (the Gap-#4 answer): point the lead CTA at the real /reference/governance-model/ page (Founding-Architect stewardship + a quarterly release-cadence table + a public change-proposal process -- it answers all three reviewer pains). The global .btn classes are AA both modes; the .section-inner scaffold + .net-* alternating-band pattern already live in network.astro. The ethos language is echoed (not copied) from narrative_ethos_public_good.md.
iii_7step.select: Append one Sparse band after band 4 (.net-run), on DEFAULT ground (alternates back from .net-run's --color-bg-alt), keeping the doctrine density arc Sparse->Medium->Medium->Dense->Sparse. Trust FACTS as non-link pills (the on-page governance scent -- visible before a click, answering the EA 'nothing on the page points there' pain) + the CTAs carry the navigation (exactly the homepage precedent). Four pills name the four facts the reviewers said were missing: 'Founding-Architect stewardship' (named steward), 'Open spec - MIT' (license), 'Public change process' (RFC/proposal process), 'Quarterly standard cadence' (release cadence). CTA pair: primary 'Read the governance model' -> /reference/governance-model/, secondary 'Join the community' -> /community/ (also feeds join-scent). One muted ethos close line below. NO public-good vault showcase (that is E5 / Movement Skeptic).
iii_7step.implement: site/src/pages/network.astro ONLY: the Sparse .net-govern band (h2 'Governed in the open' -- uses the verb the Community Lead flagged as unbacked; an ethos-threaded .section-subtitle; a <ul class=gov-signals> of 4 non-link .gov-tag fact pills; a .gov-cta with a.btn.btn-primary -> /reference/governance-model/ + a.btn.btn-secondary -> /community/; a muted .gov-ethos manifesto close line); scoped <style> for .gov-signals (centered wrap row) / .gov-tag (mirrors the homepage .meta-tag pill: mono, --color-surface, --radius-full, --color-border) / .gov-cta (centered button row) / .gov-ethos (muted, centered, max-width 38rem); .net-govern added to the shared band-padding rule + the 480px mobile padding-reduction (NOT the bg-alt group -- default ground). Header comment updated to bands 1-5. Zero new JS / motion / images.
iii_7step.re_measure: build PASS 162 pages (unchanged -- same-route addition); prebuild data regen idempotent apart from a generated_at date stamp (reverted, topology byte-identical). Lighthouse /network Perf 98 / A11y 100 / BP 100 / SEO 100 (LCP 2.1s, CLS 0, TBT 0ms) -- identical to the cycle-152 proven-perf-neutral baseline (band is below-the-fold static markup, does not touch the hero LCP element).
iii_7step.record: this JSON + cycle log in the session + mission/STATE cascade

change_summary: site/src/pages/network.astro -- appended Band 5 'Govern & trust' (Sparse, closing band): h2 'Governed in the open' + an ethos-threaded subtitle ('The network is a commons, not a silo. The standard that holds it is openly specified and openly governed -- a named steward, a public process for proposing change, and every decision on the record.') + a 4-pill .gov-signals trust strip (Founding-Architect stewardship / Open spec - MIT / Public change process / Quarterly standard cadence -- the four facts the E1 Gap-#4 reviewers named as missing) + a .gov-cta CTA pair (.btn-primary 'Read the governance model' -> /reference/governance-model/, .btn-secondary 'Join the community' -> /community/) + a muted .gov-ethos close ('Language and DNA are our shared heritage. So is context -- the accumulated understanding of a civilization, held in common and stewarded for the generations that inherit it.')

site/src/pages/network.astro <style> -- scoped .gov-signals (centered flex-wrap pill row) + .gov-tag (mirrors homepage .meta-tag: mono, --text-xs, --color-surface, --color-border, --radius-full) + .gov-cta (centered button row, wrap) + .gov-ethos (muted manifesto line, --text-base, max-width 38rem, centered); .net-govern added to the shared band-padding/overflow group + the 480px mobile padding-reduction group (default ground -- NOT the bg-alt group, so it alternates back from .net-run)
site/src/pages/network.astro header comment -- updated from 'bands 1-3 ... 152/153 deferred' to 'cycles 151-153, bands 1-5 ... Sparse->Medium->Medium->Dense->Sparse'

The pills state real facts, not aspirations; no invented governance dashboard. Gap #4 answered PARTIAL by design (the full org surface is E3).
doctrine_compliance.two_fighting_colors: PASS -- cyan --color-link (inherited link signal) + purple --color-primary (the primary CTA via the global .btn-primary) -- the same pair the rest of /network uses; the .gov-tag pills are neutral (--color-surface / --color-border / --color-text-muted); amber accent untouched; no new colors

doctrine_compliance.dual_audience: PASS -- developers read Founding-Architect stewardship + open spec/MIT + public change process + quarterly cadence + the governance-model link; newcomers read 'the network is a commons, not a silo ... governed in the open' + the shared-heritage ethos close

notes: Cycle 153 CLOSES the /network page -- all 5 design-spec bands now ship (Sparse hero -> Medium real-relationships -> Medium what-is-an-aDNA-computer -> Dense run-a-node -> Sparse govern-&-trust). The one judgment call was Gap-#4 SCOPE: the E1 reviewers asked for a lot (security contact, decision logs, named maintainers, RFC process), but the design spec scopes band 5 as a PARTIAL trust-anchor (the full org surface is E3). Resolution: surface the four facts the reviewers most named (steward / license / change process / cadence) as on-page pills + one click to the real governance-model page that substantiates them -- enough to clear 'is this governed?' without building the E3 org surface early. Reuse-over-invention held all the way: the homepage .the-standard band IS the trust-anchor pattern, so band 5 is that device tilted to governance, and the global .btn classes carry AA-both-modes for free. The ethos close is deliberately ONE muted line -- the doctrine's 'shown-not-preached' + the ~55/45 dial mean the governance facts do the persuading and the manifesto line just lands the page. No public-good vault showcase (E5 owns that; avoided scope creep). Mission /network build arc complete; M5.11/E4 moves to relationship-block polish (c154) then measure/iterate (c155-158) then decadal close (c159).
